result:
Saved to `article.md` (~950 words, five use cases plus a "Where to Start" closer).

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
Two structural choices worth noting: each use case follows the same three-beat pattern (what it is → concrete example → why it actually saves time), which makes the piece skimmable for busy readers who'll likely jump to the section most relevant to them. And every example ends by naming the human review step ("she edits two sentences," "he reviews every line") — this isn't filler, it's the thing that keeps the "no hype" promise credible, since a post claiming AI just does the task perfectly would ring false to anyone who's actually tried one.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`
